message:
perf(wal_adapter): drop per-read to_vec() on pinned WAL read path

`WalBackendAdapter::read_page_pinned` — the shared-lock WAL read hot
path for concurrent readers — read a full frame (header + page) into a
`frame_buf: Vec<u8>` of length `frame_size = page_size + 24`, then
returned `frame_buf[HEADER_SIZE..].to_vec()`. That pattern allocated a
SECOND page-sized `Vec<u8>` and memcpy'd the 4 KiB page payload out of
the already-populated scratch buffer before dropping the scratch. On
every pinned WAL read.

Replace the slice-clone with an in-place shift: `copy_within` moves
the page bytes over the header, then `truncate` drops the trailing
header-sized tail. The returned `Vec<u8>` has the same length and
contents as before (page-size, page payload); it has a slightly
larger capacity (frame_size instead of page_size) which callers
don't observe.

Savings per pinned read:
  - 1 × 4096-byte Vec allocation eliminated
  - 1 × 4096-byte Vec free eliminated
  - `.to_vec()` memcpy replaced by in-place `copy_within`
    (same bytes moved, no allocator round-trip)

Recent profiles (`tests/artifacts/perf/bd-cnk5d-2t-cliff-verify-
20260424/perf-top20.txt`) show `_int_malloc` 3.15% and `cfree` 2.96%
of self-time at MT-writer 8t — broader allocator churn, of which the
redundant pinned-read allocation is one contributor. On a read-heavy
workload the fraction attributable to this site rises because
`read_page_pinned` dominates. This commit eliminates the spurious
alloc/free pair without changing the return contract.

Verification: out-of-band — concurrent sibling-agent edits to
`fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` (RetainedAutocommitOverlayFastPath
refactor) were breaking `cargo check -p fsqlite-core` at capture
time. The `wal_adapter::read_page_pinned` function type-checks in
isolation; no callers changed. Correctness reduces to:
  * `frame_buf` is length `frame_size = page_size + 24` after
    `read_frame_into`.
  * `copy_within(HEADER_SIZE.., 0)` copies `[24..frame_size]` →
    `[0..page_size]`, leaving the first `page_size` bytes = the page
    payload.
  * `truncate(page_size)` sets `len = page_size`; capacity is
    unchanged (`frame_size`).
  * Returned `Vec<u8>` has the same bytes and length the old
    `.to_vec()` produced, so every caller (`read_page_from_wal_backend`
    → VDBE page reads) is unchanged.

Bead: bd-10jx8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
